        -- The derivative of a constant function is zero.
        have h₄ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ³ => ((Real.rotationMatrix x.1).comp (Real.rotationMatrix x.2 x.3)) S) (0 : ℝ³) x := by
          convert hasDerivAt_const x (((Real.rotationMatrix x.1).comp (Real.rotationMatrix x.2 x.3)) S) using 1 <;> simp [hasDerivAt_const]
        exact h₄
      exact h₃
    -- Use the fact that the derivative exists and is continuous to prove that the function is differentiable.
    have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => ((Real.rotationMatrix x.1).comp (Real.rotationMatrix x.2 x.3)) S) := by
      refine' differentiable_of_hasDerivAt fun x => _
      have h₄ : HasDerivAt (fun x : ℝ³ => ((Real.rotationMatrix x.1).comp (Real.rotationMatrix x.2 x.3)) S) (0 : ℝ³) x := h₂ x
      convert h₄ using 1 <;> simp
    exact h₃

exact differentiable_pow 3
  
  have h_id : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ => x) := by
    exact differentiable_id
  
  have h_sub : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ => x ^ 3 - x) := by
    apply Differentiable.sub h_pow h_id
  
  have h_main : Differentiable ℝ f := by
    rw [hf]
    exact h_sub
  
  exact h_main

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => (x 1 : ℝ) := by
    apply Differentiable.comp (differentiable_id : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => x))
    apply differentiable_const.inr
    <;> simp [Function.const]
  have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => (x 2 : ℝ) := by
    apply Differentiable.comp (differentiable_id : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => x))
    apply differentiable_const.inr
    <;> simp [Function.const]
  have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => (x 0 : ℝ) := by
    apply Differentiable.comp (differentiable_id : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => x))
    apply differentiable_const.inr
    <;> simp [Function.const]
  have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 0) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
    apply Differentiable.differentiableAt
    apply Fintype.det (fun x : ℝ => ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)
    <;> infer_instance
  -- Prove that the composition of linear maps is differentiable.
  have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
    have h₅₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x 1 : ℝ)) := h₁
    have h₅₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x 2 : ℝ)) := h₂
    -- Use the fact that the rotation matrix is differentiable in the angles.
    apply Differentiable.differentiableAt
    apply Fintype.det (fun x : ℝ => ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)
    <;> simp_all [rotM]
    <;>
    (try infer_instance) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try
      funext x
      simp [Real.differentiableAt_sin, Real.differentiableAt_cos]) <;>
    (try
      {
        intro x
        apply Differentiable.differentiableAt
        <;>
        (try simp_all [rotM]) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try
          {
            differentiableAt_id
            <;>
            differentiableAt_const _
          })
      })
  -- Prove that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
  have h₆ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 0) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³).smulRight (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
    -- Use the fact that the product of differentiable functions is differentiable.
    apply Differentiable.mul (h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 0) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)))
      (h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)))
  -- Finally, prove that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
  have h₇ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => ((rotR (x 0)).smulRight (rotM (x 1) (x 2))) S) := by
    apply Differentiable.comp (h₆)
    apply differentiable_id
  -- Simplify the expression to match the form in the problem statement.
  simp_all [Matrix.mul_smul, Matrix.comp_apply, Matrix.mul_apply, Fin.sum_univ_succ,
    Real.pi_pos.le]
  <;>
  (try
    {
      -- Handle any remaining differentiability proofs by showing compositions are differentiable.
      apply Differentiable.of_locally_differentiable
      <;>
      (try
        {
          filter_upwards [isOpen_univ.mem_nhds (Set.mem_univ _)] with x hx
          <;>
          infer_instance
        })
    })

apply Differentiable.add
  · apply Differentiable.add
    · -- Prove differentiability of the first term: cos(x₁) * (cos(x₂) * S - sin(x₂) * S)
      apply Differentiable.mul
      · -- cos(x₁)
        exact Differentiable.cos (differentiable_fst)
      · -- cos(x₂) * S - sin(x₂) * S
        apply Differentiable.sub
        · -- cos(x₂) * S
          apply Differentiable.mul
          · -- cos(x₂)
            exact Differentiable.cos (differentiable_snd)
          · -- S
            exact differentiable_const
        · -- sin(x₂) * S
          apply Differentiable.mul
          · -- sin(x₂)
            exact Differentiable.sin (differentiable_snd)
          · -- S
            exact differentiable_const
    · -- Prove differentiability of the second term: x₃ * sin(x₁) * S
      apply Differentiable.mul
      · -- x₃ * sin(x₁)
        apply Differentiable.mul
        · -- x₃
          exact differentiable_of_fst (by simp)
        · -- sin(x₁)
          exact Differentiable.sin (differentiable_fst)
      · -- S
        exact differentiable_const
  · -- Prove differentiability of the third term: sin(x₁) * S
    apply Differentiable.mul
    · -- sin(x₁)
      exact Differentiable.sin (differentiable_fst)
    · -- S
      exact differentiable_const

exact (differentiable_id : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => x)).comp _ (differentiable_id)
    · -- The projection function (x : Fin 3 → ℝ) ↦ x 0 is differentiable.
      exact differentiable_id

  have h2 : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => (x.ofLp 1 : ℝ) := by
    apply differentiable_of_composition
    · -- The projection function (x : ℝ³) ↦ x.1 is differentiable.
      exact (differentiable_id : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => x)).comp _ (differentiable_id)
    · -- The projection function (x : Fin 3 → ℝ) ↦ x 1 is differentiable.
      exact differentiable_id

  have h3 : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => (x.ofLp 2 : ℝ) := by
    apply differentiable_of_composition
    · -- The projection function (x : ℝ³) ↦ x.1 is differentiable.
      exact (differentiable_id : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => x)).comp _ (differentiable_id)
    · -- The projection function (x : Fin 3 → ℝ) ↦ x 2 is differentiable.
      exact differentiable_id

  have h4 : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x.ofLp 0) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) := by
    have h4₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ => (rotR x : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
      -- Prove that the function x ↦ rotR x is differentiable using known differentiability of trigonometric functions.
      apply Differentiable.of_clocally_differentiable
      refine' clocally_differentiable_of_locally_differentiable _
      -- Use the fact that cosine and sine functions are locally differentiable.
      refine' metric.ball_clocally_differentiable ℝ (Continuous.restrict _ _) fun x => _
      · -- Prove that the function is continuous.
        exact Continuous.continuousOn (Continuous.comp (rotR.continuous) continuous_id)
      · -- Prove that the function is differentiable at each point in the open set.
        filter_upwards [isOpen_Iio.mem_nhds (by linarith : (1 : ℝ) < 1 + 1)] with y hy
        apply DifferentiableAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq (differentiableAt_id.const_mul 1)
        <;> simp_all [rotR]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> aesop
    -- Use the composition of differentiable functions to conclude differentiability.
    have h4₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x.ofLp 0 : ℝ)) := h1
    exact h4₁.comp h4₂

  have h5 : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) := by
    have h5₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x y : ℝ => (rotM x y : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
      -- Prove that the function (x,y) ↦ rotM x y is differentiable using known differentiability of trigonometric functions.
      apply Differentiable.of_clocally_differentiable
      refine' clocally_differentiable_of_locally_differentiable _
      -- Use the fact that cosine and sine functions are locally differentiable.
      refine' isOpen_Iio.prod.isOpen.prod.isOpen.mem_nhds _ fun x y z => _
      · -- Prove that the function is continuous.
        exact Product.isOpen_Iio.prod isOpen_Iio.prod isOpen_Iio |>.mem_nhds (by norm_num)
      · -- Prove that the function is differentiable at each point in the open set.
        filter_upwards [isOpen_Iio.mem_nhds (by linarith : (1 : ℝ) < 1 + 1),
          isOpen_Iio.mem_nhds (by linarith : (1 : ℝ) < 1 + 1),
          isOpen_Iio.mem_nhds (by linarith : (1 : ℝ) < 1 + 1)] with x hx y hy z hz
        -- Use the fact that cosine and sine functions are locally differentiable.
        apply DifferentiableAt.congr_of_eventuallyEq (differentiableAt_id.const_mul 1)
        <;> simp_all [rotM]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> ring_nf
        <;> aesop
    -- Use the composition of differentiable functions to conclude differentiability.
    have h5₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => ((x.ofLp 1 : ℝ), (x.ofLp 2 : ℝ))) := by
      -- Prove that the function x ↦ (x.ofLp 1, x.ofLp 2) is differentiable.
      have h5₂₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x.ofLp 1 : ℝ)) := h2
      have h5₂₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x.ofLp 2 : ℝ)) := h3
      exact h5₂₁.prod h5₂₂
    -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
    have h5₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) :=
      h5₁.comp h5₂
    exact h5₃

  have h6 : Differentiable ℝ fun x : ℝ³ => ((rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2))) S := by
    have h6₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x.ofLp 0) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h4
    have h6₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h5
    have h6₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2))) := by
      -- Prove that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
      have h6₃₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x.ofLp 0) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h4
      have h6₃₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2) : ℝ³ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h5
      -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
      apply Differentiable.comp
      · exact h6₃₁
      · exact h6₃₂
    -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
    have h6₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => ((rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2))) S) := by
      have h6₄₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2))) := h6₃
      have h6₄₂ : S ∈ {S : ℝ³ | True} := by trivial
      -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable.
      apply Differentiable.comp
      · exact h6₄₁
      · -- Prove that the constant function S is differentiable.
        exact differentiable_const
    exact h6₄

  apply h6

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S) := by
    have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S) := by
      -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
      have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x 1, x 2, x 0) : ℝ³ → ℝ × ℝ × ℝ) := by
        apply Differentiable.prod
        <;> (try apply Differentiable.fst)
        <;> (try apply Differentiable.snd)
        <;> (try apply Differentiable.fst)
        <;> (try apply Differentiable.snd)
        <;> exact differentiable_id
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (p : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ) : ℝ³ => (rotM p.1 p.2 : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) p.2.1 S) := by
        -- Prove that the function is differentiable
        have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (p : ℝ × ℝ × ℝ) : ℝ³ => (rotM p.1 p.2 : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) p.2.1 S) := by
          apply Differentiable.congr _ (fun p => rfl)
          apply Differentiable.smooth_of_mem
          apply F.smooth
        exact h₅
      have h₆ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S) :=
        h₄.comp h₃
      exact h₆
    exact h₂
  have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
    have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
      -- Prove that the function is differentiable
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) => x 1 : ℝ³ → ℝ) := by
        apply Differentiable.fst
      have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (a : ℝ) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR a : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
        apply Differentiable.smooth_of_mem
        apply F.smooth
      have h₆ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
        apply Differentiable.comp h₅ h₄
      exact h₆
    exact h₃
  -- Combine the differentiability of the two parts to prove the overall differentiability
  have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) ((rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S)) := by
    have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h₂
    have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S) := h₁
    have h₆ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) ((rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S)) := by
      -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
      have h₇ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) => (x : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := by
        apply Differentiable.id
      have h₈ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) ((rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
        have h₉ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S) := h₁
        have h₁₀ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h₂
        have h₁₁ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h₂
        have h₁₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) ((rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S)) := by
          -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
          have h₁₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S) := h₁
          have h₁₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³)) := h₂
          -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
          have h₁₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => (rotR (x 1) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) ((rotM (x 1) (x 2) : ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ →L[ℝ] ℝ³) (x 0) S)) := by
            -- Use the fact that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
            apply Differentiable.comp (h₁₄) h₁₃
          exact h₁₅
        exact h₁₂
      exact h₈
    exact h₆
  exact h₃

-- each of which is differentiable.
  -- Thus, the composition is differentiable.
  have h4 : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2))) := by
    apply Differentiable.comp
    · -- Differentiability of the composition of `rotR` and `rotM`
      apply Differentiable.comp
      · -- Differentiability of `rotR`
        -- `rotR` is differentiable as it is a linear function.
        intro x
        exact differentiableAt_id'
      · -- Differentiability of `rotM`
        intro x
        have h5 : DifferentiableAt ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2)) x := by
          have h6 : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x.ofLp 1 : ℝ)) := h2
          have h7 : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => (x.ofLp 2 : ℝ)) := h3
          have h8 : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) => rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2)) := by
            apply Differentiable.forall
            intro x
            apply Differentiable.comp (differentiable_const _)
            exact differentiable_const _
          exact h8.differentiableAt
        exact h5
    · -- Differentiability of the identity function
      intro x
      exact differentiableAt_id'
  -- Since the composition is differentiable, the function `fun x => ((rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2))) S` is differentiable.
  have h5 : Differentiable ℝ (fun x : ℝ³ => ((rotR (x.ofLp 0)).comp (rotM (x.ofLp 1) (x.ofLp 2))) S) := by
    apply Differentiable.comp (h4.differentiableAt)
    exact differentiableAt_id'
  exact h5

have h₁ : Differentiable ℝ fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => _root_.rotR (x 0) (_root_.rotM (x 1) (x 2) S) := by
    apply Differentiable.comp
    · -- Prove that rotR is differentiable
      have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (θ : ℝ) : ℝ³ → ℝ³ => _root_.rotR θ) := by
        -- The function θ ↦ rotR θ is differentiable because it's a trigonometric function
        have h₃ : ∀ i j : Fin 3, Differentiable ℝ (fun (θ : ℝ) : ℝ => (_root_.rotR θ i j)) := by
          intro i j
          fin_cases i <;> fin_cases j <;>
          (try decide) <;>
          (try {
            simp [rotR]
            <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.const_mul; apply Differentiable.cos }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.sin }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.const_mul; apply Differentiable.sin }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.const_mul; apply Differentiable.cos })
          }) <;>
          (try {
            simp [rotR]
            <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.cos }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.sin })
          })
        -- Each entry of θ ↦ rotR θ is differentiable, so the entire function is differentiable
        exact by
          infer_instance
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ => x 0) := by
        apply Differentiable.apply
        · exact differentiable_univ
        · exact simpa using projection_differentiable 0
      -- Use the composition of differentiable functions to show that the composition is differentiable
      have h₅ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ → ℝ³ => (fun (θ : ℝ) : ℝ³ → ℝ³ => _root_.rotR θ) ∘ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ => x 0)) := by
        apply Differentiable.comp
        · exact h₂
        · exact h₄
      -- Use the fact that the composition is differentiable to conclude that it's differentiable
      have h₆ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ → ℝ³ => (fun (θ : ℝ) : ℝ³ → ℝ³ => _root_.rotR θ) (x 0)) := by
        convert h₅ using 1 <;> simp [Function.comp]
      convert h₆ using 1 <;> simp [Function.comp]
    · -- Prove that x ↦ rotM (x 1) (x 2) S is differentiable
      have h₂ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => _root_.rotM (x 1) (x 2) S) := by
        -- The function x ↦ rotM (x 1) (x 2) S is differentiable because it's a combination of trigonometric functions
        have h₃ : ∀ i : Fin 3, Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ => (_root_.rotM (x 1) (x 2) S) i) := by
          intro i
          fin_cases i <;>
          (try simp_all [rotM, Fin.sum_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_zero]) <;>
          (try {
            apply Differentiable.add
            <;>
            (try {
              apply Differentiable.mul
              <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.cos }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.sin }) <;>
              (try {
                apply Differentiable.comp
                <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.const_inc }) <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.id }) <;>
                (try {
                  apply Differentiable.add
                  <;>
                  (try { apply Differentiable.const_mul }) <;>
                  (try { apply Differentiable.id })
                })
              })
            }) <;>
            (try { apply Differentiable.cos }) <;>
            (try { apply Differentiable.sin }) <;>
            (try {
              apply Differentiable.comp
              <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.const_inc }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.id }) <;>
              (try {
                apply Differentiable.add
                <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.const_mul }) <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.id })
              })
            })
          }) <;>
          (try {
            apply Differentiable.sub
            <;>
            (try {
              apply Differentiable.mul
              <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.cos }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.sin }) <;>
              (try {
                apply Differentiable.comp
                <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.const_inc }) <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.id }) <;>
                (try {
                  apply Differentiable.add
                  <;>
                  (try { apply Differentiable.const_mul }) <;>
                  (try { apply Differentiable.id })
                })
              })
            }) <;>
            (try { apply Differentiable.cos }) <;>
            (try { apply Differentiable.sin }) <;>
            (try {
              apply Differentiable.comp
              <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.const_inc }) <;>
              (try { apply Differentiable.id }) <;>
              (try {
                apply Differentiable.add
                <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.const_mul }) <;>
                (try { apply Differentiable.id })
              })
            })
          })
        -- Use the fact that each component is differentiable to show that the entire function is differentiable
        exact by
          infer_instance
      -- Use the fact that the composition with a differentiable function is differentiable to conclude that it's differentiable
      have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => _root_.rotM (x 1) (x 2) S) := by
        exact h₂
      exact h₄
  have h₂ : ∀ (i : Fin 3), Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ => (_root_.rotR (x 0) (_root_.rotM (x 1) (x 2) S) i)) := by
    intro i
    have h₃ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ³ => _root_.rotR (x 0) (_root_.rotM (x 1) (x 2) S)) := h₁
    have h₄ : Differentiable ℝ (fun (x : ℝ³) : ℝ => (_root_.rotR (x 0) (_root_.rotM (x 1) (x 2) S) i)) := by
      apply Differentiable.apply
      · exact h₃
      · exact simpa using projection_differentiable i
    exact h₄
  -- Use the fact that each component is differentiable to show that the entire function is differentiable
  exact by
    infer_instance

· -- Prove that the composition of differentiable functions is differentiable
    apply Differentiable.comp
    · -- Differentiability of the inner function
      apply Differentiable.comp
      · -- Differentiability of the outer function with respect to the first argument
        exact differentiable_id
      · -- Differentiability of the function with respect to the second argument
        intro _ hx
        exact (differentiable_const.rpow_const (differentiable_id)) _
    · -- Differentiability of the function with respect to the third argument
      intro _ hx
      apply Differentiable.ext <;> simp_all [Function.funext_iff, LinearMap.comp_apply]
      <;> continuity
  · -- Differentiability of the constant function
    intro _ hx
    apply Differentiable.ext <;> simp_all [Function.funext_iff, LinearMap.comp_apply]
    <;> continuity
